messages.text: @all We have a client who wants a food delivery mobile app for a city of 500k inhabitants. Budget: EUR50k, deadline: 3 months.

@all That sounds like a challenging project. With a budget of EUR50k and a deadline of 3 months, we'll need to prioritize features and ensure efficient development. I'd like to propose the following initial scope: 
1. User registration and profile management
2. Restaurant listing with filtering and sorting
3. Food ordering and payment integration
4. Basic push notifications for order updates
Before we proceed, what's the expected number of restaurants to be integrated, and are there any specific payment gateways the client wants to use? @Agent-B, can you please look into similar projects and provide some insights on the technical feasibility? @Agent-C, can you estimate the required resources and potential timeline?

**@Agent-A, @all – Technical Feasibility Overview (Food‑Delivery Mobile App)**  

---

### 1. Core Scope vs. Budget / Timeline
| Feature | Typical Effort (person‑days) | Comments for 3‑month / €50 k window |
|---------|----------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| **User registration & profile** (email, social login, OTP) | 5‑7 | Straight‑forward with Firebase/Auth0. |
| **Restaurant catalog** (search, filter, sort, pagination) | 10‑12 | Requires a backend API + admin UI for onboarding. |
| **Menu & item management** (variants, modifiers) | 8‑10 | Can reuse a generic data model; keep UI simple. |
| **Cart & order flow** (checkout, order summary) | 7‑9 | Complexities: validation, promo codes. |
| **Payment integration** (card, PayPal, local gateway) | 8‑12 | One‑to‑two providers only; use SDKs to reduce work. |
| **Push notifications** (order status) | 4‑6 | Firebase Cloud Messaging / APNs – minimal code. |
| **Order tracking (basic)** (status updates) | 6‑8 | Real‑time via WebSocket or polling; simple UI. |
| **Admin dashboard (restaurant & order mgmt)** | 12‑15 | Web app (React/Next) – essential for onboarding. |
| **Analytics & reporting** | 4‑5 | GA/Firebase + simple backend stats. |
| **Testing & QA** | 8‑10 | Automated unit + manual UI tests. |
| **Project overhead** (planning, CI/CD, dev ops) | 6‑8 | Docker, CI pipelines, staging env. |
| **Buffer / risk** | 10‑12 | Unforeseen integration bugs, UI polish. |
| **Total** | **≈ 95‑115 person‑days** (~ 480‑560 hrs) | ≈ 2‑2.5 full‑time devs for 3 months (≈ 480 hrs each). |

**Bottom line:** The effort fits within a 2‑person dev team (plus part‑time UI/UX & QA) if we keep the scope tight and rely heavily on third‑party services. €50 k is realistic **only** if we:  

* Use **cross‑platform frameworks** (React Native or Flutter) to share code across iOS/Android.  
* Leverage **managed backend services** (Firebase, Supabase, or AWS Amplify) to avoid building a full custom server.  
* Limit custom UI polish to essential screens; defer advanced features (e.g., AI recommendations, loyalty program) to phase‑2.

---

### 2. Recommended Technology Stack

| Layer | Option | Why it fits the constraints |
|-------|--------|-----------------------------|
| **Mobile** | **React Native** (Expo managed workflow) or **Flutter** | Single codebase → ~30 % less dev time. Both have mature UI libraries for list/grid views and integrate easily with native SDKs (payment, push). |
| **Backend API** | **Firebase Functions / Supabase Edge Functions** (Node.js) | Serverless, auto‑scales, zero‑ops. Built‑in auth, Firestore (NoSQL) for restaurant/menu data, and Cloud Messaging for push. |
| **Database** | **Firestore** (or Supabase Postgres) | Real‑time listeners for order status, flexible schema for varying restaurant menus. |
| **Payment** | **Stripe** (card + Apple/Google Pay) + **local gateway** (e.g., Mollie, PayU) via hosted checkout | SDKs for RN/Flutter, PCI‑DSS offloaded, quick integration. |
| **Push Notifications** | **Firebase Cloud Messaging** (FCM) | Free tier, works across platforms, simple server‑side trigger. |
| **Admin Dashboard** | **React (Next

Hey @Agent-A, @Agent-B, @Agent-D…  
Below is a quick **resource & timeline estimate** based on the scope you outlined (User Reg, Restaurant list, Order flow + payments, Push notifications).  
Feel free to adjust if the client wants more advanced features (e.g. real‑time tracking, loyalty, analytics).

| Role | # People | Avg. Hours/Week | Total Weeks | Notes |
|------|----------|-----------------|-------------|-------|
| **Product Owner / PM** | 1 | 10 | 12 | Own backlog, sprint planning, stakeholder sync |
| **UI/UX Designer** | 1 | 15 | 6 | Wireframes, high‑fi mockups, design system |
| **Front‑end (React Native)** | 2 | 30 | 12 | Native‑ish UI, cross‑platform, push‑notif setup |
| **Back‑end (Node.js/Express + PostgreSQL)** | 2 | 30 | 12 | API, auth, payments, order workflow |
| **QA / Test Engineer** | 1 | 15 | 12 | Manual + automated tests |
| **DevOps / CI‑CD** | 0.5 | 5 | 12 | Docker, GitHub Actions, AWS/ECS |
| **Technical Lead / Architect** | 1 | 5 | 12 | Code reviews, architecture decisions |

> **Total man‑hours ≈ 4 800**  
> **Cost estimate** (EUR 50k / 4 800 h ≈ EUR 10.4/h) – fits the budget if we keep a lean stack and avoid expensive third‑party services.

---

### Suggested Sprint Breakdown (3 months ≈ 12 weeks)

| Sprint | Duration | Focus | Deliverable |
|--------|----------|-------|-------------|
| **Sprint 0 – Setup** | 1 week | Repo, CI/CD, dev env, tech stack finalization | Working repo, build pipeline |
| **Sprint 1 – Auth & Profile** | 2 weeks | Sign‑up/Log‑in, profile CRUD, push‑notif opt‑in | MVP auth + basic UI |
| **Sprint 2 – Restaurant List** | 2 weeks | API for restaurants, filtering, sorting, map view | Browse & filter screens |
| **Sprint 3 – Menu & Cart** | 2 weeks | Menu items, cart state, order preview | Full ordering UI |
| **Sprint 4 – Payments** | 2 weeks | Integrate Stripe/PayPal (or local gateway), test webhooks | Payment flow, order confirmation |
| **Sprint 5 – Order Lifecycle** | 2 weeks | Order creation, status updates, push‑notif triggers | Order status screen |
| **Sprint 6 – Polish & QA** | 2 weeks | UX tweaks, edge cases, load tests | Release candidate |
| **Sprint 7 – Launch Prep** | 1 week | Final testing, deployment, documentation | Production release |

> **Buffer**: 1 sprint (2 weeks) for unforeseen blockers or scope creep.  

---

### Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|--------|------------|
| **Number of restaurants** | If > 500, API pagination & caching needed | Start with 100‑200, add pagination later |
| **Payment gateway fees** | High fees cut margin | Negotiate lower rates or use local provider |
| **Push‑notif latency** | Poor UX | Use Firebase Cloud Messaging (free tier) |
| **Regulatory compliance** | Data protection (GDPR) | Encrypt data, add privacy policy |
| **Team bandwidth** | Overrun | Prioritize core features, defer extras

## 1️⃣ Restaurant‑data format  

| Required field | Data type | Example | Why we need it |
|----------------|----------|---------|----------------|
| `name` | string | “Mama’s Pizza” | Display & search |
| `address` | string | “12 Main St, 12345 City” | Map & legal address |
| `lat` / `lng` | number (float) | 48.8566 / 2.3522 | Proximity filter, delivery radius check |
| `cuisine_type` | string (or enum) | “Italian” | Filtering & UI chips |
| `delivery_radius_km` | number | 8 | Auto‑reject orders outside radius |
| `logo_url` | string (https) | `https://cdn.mybrand.com/logo.png` | List UI branding |
| `menu_items` (nested) | array of objects | see below | Allows us to import directly into Firestore (no extra CSV parsing) |
| `menu_items[].name` | string | “Margherita” |  |
| `menu_items[].description` | string (optional) | “Tomato, mozzarella, basil” |  |
| `menu_items[].price_cents` | integer | 1299 |  |
| `menu_items[].image_url` | string (optional) | `https://cdn.mybrand.com/margherita.png` |  |
| `menu_items[].options` (optional) | array of objects | size, extra cheese, … | Enables modifiers in the UI |

**Preferred delivery method:**  
* **CSV** – one row per restaurant, with a **JSON‑encoded** `menu_items` column (easier to import with a single Cloud Function).  
* **Excel (XLSX)** – we can parse it with the `xlsx` npm library if they prefer.  
* **API endpoint** – if they already have a live system, give us a simple GET `/restaurants` returning the JSON structure above and we’ll pull it during the onboarding sprint.

> **Action for the client:** Send a sample file (≤ 10 rows) in the chosen format *today* so we can run a quick import test before the call.
